channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: When boto3 doesn't have it (yet), you write it: a realtime speech-to-speech story in Python
item.description: At a meetup's networking session, someone dropped: "the new speech-to-speech feature in Teams is really cool". Microsoft Teams added the interpreter agent with realtime AI-powered speech-to-speech...

item.title: Zero-Trust RAG: Defeating the Shared Private Link Deadlock in Azure Terraform
item.description: Your Terraform pipeline is green. The deployment completes without errors. You grab a coffee. Ten minutes later, you test your new Enterprise RAG application. It throws a 403 Forbidden. You open...

item.title: You Can't Co-Design What You Don't Operate
item.description: An article circulating this week argues that faculty AI buy-in in higher education is a human factors engineering problem. The framing is correct. The path the piece describes skips the only two...

item.title: Counting tokens is dumb. So we built a free metric for AI proficiency.
item.description: We’ve been trying to figure out a real answer to a question that keeps coming up: how do you measure whether someone is actually good at Claude Code, Codex, and the other AI coding tools? Not "do...

item.title: Musk’s xAI is being sued over its data center generators. Now, it’s buying $2.8B more.
item.description: Elon Muks's xAI said it will buy $2.8 billion worth of natural gas turbines over the next three years, according to SpaceX's IPO filing.

item.title: Starship's Twelfth Flight Test
item.description: Article URL: https://www.spacex.com/launches/starship-flight-12 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48214558 Points: 3 # Comments: 0

item.title: Choosing the Right RAG Strategy A Complete Decision Guide to Chunking, Agentic RAG, and GraphRAG
item.description: Introduction Here is a scenario many RAG builders know well, you wire up a pipeline, load your documents, ask a question and the answer is wrong, vague, or confidently hallucinated. The...

item.title: The Egregious Cost of Compliance: One Platform's Overly Broad Restrictions
item.description: The Problem We Were Actually Solving We were actually trying to solve the classic problem of onboarding new creators. We believed that by supporting PayPal and Stripe, we'd open the door for more...

item.title: GitHub Breach via VSCode Extension, ZTE Router CVE-2026-34472, & Public Repo Secrets Leaks
item.description: GitHub Breach via VSCode Extension, ZTE Router CVE-2026-34472, & Public Repo Secrets Leaks Today's Highlights Today's security news highlights a significant GitHub internal breach traced to a...

item.title: Applied AI: From Agent Orchestration to Workflow Automation & Code Generation
item.description: Applied AI: From Agent Orchestration to Workflow Automation & Code Generation Today's Highlights This week, we explore the practical applications of AI, from leveraging LLMs for business workflow...

item.title: SQLite Journaling on SMB, TypeGraph for SQL Graphs, Cross-Engine Migrations
item.description: SQLite Journaling on SMB, TypeGraph for SQL Graphs, Cross-Engine Migrations Today's Highlights This week's highlights include advanced SQL techniques for graph queries in PostgreSQL and SQLite,...

item.title: Anthropic will pay xAI $1.25 billion per month for compute
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI surprised the AI world when it made a deal to sell compute to Anthropic. Now we know how much it's worth.

item.title: SpaceX just filed for what could be the biggest IPO ever
item.description: Elon Musk's final frontier is officially open for business now that SpaceX has formally filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC. That kicks off what could be the largest initial public offering ever...

item.title: ‘Solve all diseases,’ you say?
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. This...

item.title: We’re announcing new community investments in Missouri.
item.description: We’re helping build the state’s next-generation workforce and investing in energy programs.

item.title: OpenAI claims it solved an 80-year-old math problem — for real this time
item.description: OpenAI claims its reasoning model disproved a geometry conjecture unsolved since 1946 — and this time, the mathematicians who exposed its last embarrassing claim are backing it up.

item.title: Here are 42 of our favorite deals from REI’s massive Anniversary Sale
item.description: REI’s annual Anniversary Sale — the retailer’s biggest of the year — has arrived, bringing with it discounts on all kinds of outdoor essentials. If you’ve got a camping trip coming up, now is a...

item.title: IrisGo, a startup backed by Andrew Ng, looks to become the AI desktop buddy you never knew you needed
item.description: Initially billed as an "AI butler," Iris watches what happens on a user's desktop and automatically learns how to do tasks for them, its co-founder says.

item.title: 100 things we announced at I/O 2026
item.description: Image with the words "Ready, Set, I/O" and a colorful Gemini logo

item.title: Google publishes exploit code threatening millions of Chromium users
item.description: Google publishes exploit code before patch, reported 29 months earlier, is fixed.

item.title: I’ve tested the latest Switch 2 controllers, and this one is the best
item.description: If you choose to get an extra controller for your Nintendo Switch 2, you can spend a lot — and get a lot in return. Buying Nintendo’s own $89 Switch 2 Pro Controller, for instance, will net you...

item.title: I Gave My OpenClaw Agent a Physical Body
item.description: The coding skills of AI models are about to make it much easier to build and deploy robots.

item.title: Anthropic and OpenAI take their beef to the midterm elections
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about the car crashes piling up on a daily basis at the Washington-based intersection of technology and politics. If you're not a...

item.title: Vibe coding is coming to your phone
item.description: "There's an app for that" was the promise of the App Store from the very beginning. The app that will get your phone to do the thing you want it to? It's just a few taps away. The tagline wasn't...

item.title: Volvo is trying to put its EV stumbles in the rearview
item.description: Volvo once had ambitions to fully exit the gas car business. Now it's trying to keep its tenuous foothold in the EV market. It's not an understatement to say that Volvo's EV journey has been a...

item.title: OpenAI barrels toward IPO that may happen in September
item.description: A day after Elon Musk lost his lawsuit that threatened OpenAI's structure, leadership, and finances, OpenAI is reportedly back to prepping for its IPO.

item.title: Microsoft Open-Sources RAMPART and Clarity to Secure AI Agents During Development
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled two new open-source tools called RAMPART and Clarity to assist developers in better testing the security of artificial intelligence (AI) agents. RAMPART, short for Risk...

item.title: A new experiment brings better group meetings to Google Beam
item.description: A small group meeting featuring two in-room participants and three participants on-screen.

item.title: You can now remix other people’s YouTube Shorts with AI
item.description: Google announced a new YouTube Shorts Remix feature that lets users restyle clips or even insert themselves into other people's videos using Gemini Omni. Now, at the bottom of a YouTube Short,...

item.title: Samsung has a tentative deal with workers to avoid a memory chip strike
item.description: More than 47,000 Samsung Electronics workers were gearing up for an 18-day strike after bonus payment negotiations between the company and its union collapsed. The strike was set to start on...
